rulings.Case Title: HENRY CHAN ET AL VS. MARK W. TSANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 19, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANTS SIU CHAN AND JUSTIN TIN'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to January 13, 2022. No later than January 3, 2022 Plaintiff to file a proposed amended complaint addressing ALL issues in this Court's December 9, 2020 ruling; the amended complaint must include in the body of the complaint the final terms of the agreement between the parties and indicate the source of such terms (Ex. A, B, C or oral); clearly state if there is one contact or multiple contacts; clearly state if Plaintiff seeks a reformation of any of the agreements; clearly state facts re: statute of frauds and statute of limitations. Conclusions and vague references to multiple documents are not sufficient. No later than January 10, 2022 Defendants may file a supplemental brief addressing the proposed amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN SHIMAMOTO VS. PAULA OGI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 19, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege facts (not conclusions) supporting each element of each cause of action subject to this demurrer. The facts giving rise to liability must be within applicable statute of limitations and clearly so state. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
